bios: Learned the trade at a dealer service bay. Kept the good tools, left the paperwork.
Ran the parts counter at a breaker's yard for years. Knows what a fair price looks like.
Spent a decade under commercial vans. Says a gearbox is a gearbox.
Came up rebuilding cars for the northern rally crowd. Does not discuss the results.
Worked nights at a tyre shop through college and never quite left the trade.
Trained at a body shop two prefectures over. Reads a door panel's reflection like a newspaper.
Ex-forklift fleet mechanic. Never once been late, never once hurried.
Did a stint at a race team's satellite shop. Will not say which team.
Family ran a filling station. Grew up with a spanner in hand.
Fifteen years at a main dealer. Left when they stopped keeping the old manuals.
Retrained after the factory closed. Takes the work seriously.
Apprenticed under an old touge hand. Quiet about most of it.
Kept a fleet of taxis running on a shoestring. Frugal with parts.
Handled warranty repairs for an importer. Fluent in awkward paperwork.
Marshals at the local circuit at weekends. Knows the corners by name.
Time served at an exhaust specialist. Has opinions about back pressure.
Cut their teeth at a suspension shop. Measures twice, adjusts once.
Diesel background, petrol curiosity. Learns fast, complains little.
Left a bus depot for smaller, more interesting engines.
Restored one car properly, then could not stop.
Steady hands from years of clutch work. Never rushes a bleed.
Ran the night shift at a garage that never closed. Sleeps when the work is done.
Trained in the city, moved out for the quiet. Brought the city's standards along.
Old hand with carburettors, wary of anything with a chip in it.
